scenario: FR-017 in-flight dedup + out-of-order completion: a new hook-fired review MUST NOT launch while one is already in flight for the same lineage; and an obsolete in-flight result completing after a newer one is SUPERSEDED (digest != current), never authoritative.
note: Lineage is keyed by the review target baseline lineage (baseline_ref chain), NOT by the per-run checkpoint_id (nav-<run_id>), which is unique per fire. The in-flight registry tracks the SINGLE running review per lineage. Contract predicates under test are PURE (dedup decision + supersession by digest) — no runtime scheduling path is exercised.

runs.comment: started FIRST (t0) but completes LAST (t3) — the out-of-order case.
started SECOND (t1) but completes FIRST (t2) against the CURRENT digest.

cases.name: dedup-second-fire-does-not-launch
cases.why: run1 is already in flight for this lineage; a Stop-fired review must detect the single tracked in-flight run and wait/poll it, never launch a duplicate.

cases.name: different-lineage-fire-may-launch
cases.why: no in-flight review exists for a DIFFERENT lineage, so dedup does not block it.

cases.name: out-of-order-older-completion-superseded
cases.why: run1 completes AFTER run2 with a digest that no longer matches the current tree; its result is superseded, never a fresh block or authorization.

cases.name: current-digest-completion-authoritative
cases.why: run2's reviewed digest equals the current tree, so its terminal result is authoritative for this digest.
